metadata_json.notes: Because the environment may not have openpyxl, include both XLSX and CSV outputs and textual formula representations. Aim for minimal changes to numeric logic; most changes are documentation, naming, and additional perturbation scenarios.

Because the environment may not have openpyxl, include both XLSX and CSV outputs and textual formula representations. Aim for minimal changes to numeric logic; most changes are documentation, naming, and additional perturbation scenarios.
metadata_json.rollback_plan: If a generated XLSX becomes unreadable in downstream environments, include the CSV and the verification worksheet as authoritative numeric/text representations; revert workbook format changes and re-run OOXML assembly if necessary.
tests_and_verification_plan: Open the revised XLSX with a standard office spreadsheet application and confirm the 'delta_vs_limit_mV' column matches memo examples.
Verify that CSV artifact exists and that numeric values match worksheet outputs.
Run the verifier checklist: presence of formula-strings in verification sheet, presence of sensitivity summary showing base and threshold perturbations, and explicit cell-to-packet mappings.
Update actual_outputs.json to include the CSV path and note the verification artifacts.

actions: Add a new column in the main worksheet with header 'delta_vs_limit_mV' computed by formula: =measured_voltage_increase_mV - adjusted_limit_mV.
In the verification worksheet, include the exact cell formula as plain text (e.g., '=(Sheet1!C2 - Sheet1!D2)') and one worked numeric example for S1 and S2.
Update warranty_memo.md decision table to show 'delta_vs_limit_mV' (not 'Margin') and include the numeric worked example.
planned_changes.description: Unambiguously define delta_vs_limit_mV and update both workbook and memo.

actions: Add sensitivity scenarios that compute adjusted_limit_mV when base_limit_mV = 60*(1 +/- 0.01) and 60*(1 +/- 0.05).
Add scenarios that compute start-threshold = 500*(1 +/- 0.01) and 500*(1 +/- 0.05) and recompute start_adjust_mV and adjusted_limit_mV accordingly.
Create a compact summary table listing stacks and whether label changes under each perturbation; include change counts and highlight any flips.
planned_changes.description: Extend sensitivity perturbations to include base limit (60 mV) and starts-threshold (500) at +/-1% and +/-5%, and summarize label changes.

actions: Add a 'verification' worksheet that lists all key formulas as plain text and maps each data row to its task.json packet source with explicit cell references (e.g., 'S1: sheet1!A2 hours = task.json S1 hours=6200').
Export the primary calculation table to [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] with columns: stack_id,hours,starts,current_density,measured_delta_mV,adjusted_limit_mV,delta_vs_limit_mV,abuse_flag,label.
Mention the CSV and verification worksheet in warranty_memo.md and explain how to use them for verification without openpyxl.
planned_changes.description: Add verification-friendly artifacts for tool-agnostic review.

actions: Ensure agent_trace.json final steps clearly show the successful OOXML generation command and list the files written with their relative paths.
If any scoring/diagnostic fields are included in memo or trace, ensure names are not ambiguous (document 'artifact_contract_score' vs 'final_score').
planned_changes.description: Address small consistency/naming issues in trace and memo.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve minor semantic ambiguities (delta sign/label), extend sensitivity checks to perturb required thresholds, and improve verifier friendliness given environment preview limitations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Good deliverables: both spreadsheet and memo were produced, formulas implemented, row-level citations present, and S2 abuse/fail was correctly detected. Minor semantic issues remain: (1) the 'Margin' / delta field in the memo appears to use an inverted sign or ambiguous naming relative to the rubric's expected delta_vs_limit_mV = measured_voltage_increase_mV - adjusted_limit_mV; (2) the sensitivity analysis as documented does not explicitly show perturbation of the base 60 mV limit or the starts-threshold value (500) at the required +/-1% and +/-5% levels; (3) automated verification was limited because the environment previewer could not open the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable). Addressing these will make the deliverable fully verifiable and reduce reviewer friction.
metadata_json.rollback_plan: If a generated XLSX becomes unreadable in downstream environments, include the CSV and the verification worksheet as authoritative numeric/text representations; revert workbook format changes and re-run OOXML assembly if necessary.

Verify that CSV artifact exists and that numeric values match worksheet outputs.
Run the verifier checklist: presence of formula-strings in verification sheet, presence of sensitivity summary showing base and threshold perturbations, and explicit cell-to-packet mappings.
Update actual_outputs.json to include the CSV path and note the verification artifacts.
